I'll search for all items simultaneously to check their existence.
Based on my web search results, I can now analyze all five items. For Items 1, 3, and 4, I was unable to retrieve search results due to the tool call limit, but I can reason from the provided error details and available evidence.

Here is my analysis:

**ITEM 0:** 
The paper "Vision Matters: Simple Visual Perturbations Can Boost Multimodal Math Reasoning" was published on arXiv on June 12, 2025, with ArXiv ID 2506.09736, authored by Yuting Li, Lai Wei, Kaipeng Zheng, Jingyuan Huang, Linghe Kong, Lichao Sun, and Weiran Huang.
 The paper is clearly real. 
The title discrepancy noted in the error details — "Revisiting Visual Understanding in Multimodal Reasoning through a Lens of Image Perturbation" — corresponds to a later updated version of the same paper.
 The cited title and authors match the original v1, making this a real paper with metadata reflecting an updated version.

**ITEM 1:** No search results were returned for "Modomodo: Learning mixture-of-datasets with reinforcement learning for multimodal reasoning." However, the error details confirm that ArXiv ID 2505.24871 points to a real paper titled "MoDoMoDo: Multi-Domain Data Mixtures for Multimodal LLM Reinforcement Learning," but with a completely different author list (none of the 6 cited authors match the 10 actual authors). The cited title appears to be a loosely paraphrased/hallucinated version of the real paper's title.

**ITEM 2:** 
ArXiv ID 2503.07365 corresponds to the real paper "MM-Eureka: Exploring Visual Aha Moment with Rule-based Large-scale Reinforcement Learning" by Fanqing Meng and 13 other authors.
 The cited title ("Mm-eureka: Exploring visual aha moment with rule-based large-scale reinforcement learning") is a near-exact match with only minor capitalization differences, and the error details confirm only one author is missing (Tiancheng Han). This is a real paper with minor metadata errors.

**ITEM 3 & 4:** No search results were retrieved. However, the error details for both indicate that the ArXiv IDs point to real papers with closely related (not completely different) titles — "SATORI-R1: Incentivizing Multimodal Reasoning through Explicit Visual Anchoring" and "Perception-R1: Advancing Multimodal Reasoning Capabilities of MLLMs via Visual Perception Reward" — suggesting these are real papers whose cited titles are slight paraphrases/variants of the actual titles.
